mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices captured by CPIAUCSL signal persistent inflation that erodes real earnings expectations and prompts tighter monetary policy anticipation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL is rising month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing three-month window signals weakening labor demand that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal a restrictive monetary choke on consumer demand, amplifying negative S&P 500 equity returns beyond the effect of either series in isolation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS shows no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 1.5) over their main effects in predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) generates positive S&P 500 returns only when the economy operates below full employment as measured by an unemployment rate above 4.5%, indicating demand-responsive supply expansion; when unemployment is lower, supply gains are already priced in and the signal fades.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, positive month-over-month shocks to INDPRO do not produce positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect accelerating residential investment and forward demand for construction, durable goods, and consumer credit, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing momentum and rising capacity utilization, which translates into positive near-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations inflect upward.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining retail sales (RSAFS) and falling payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly confirms a broad-based consumer demand contraction that exceeds the predictive power of either series in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to produce negative average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,150 thousand units, it signals a contraction in residential investment that spills over into durable goods orders and construction employment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,150, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observed months.
